Reference Catalog

Browse the ports catalog directly in the website.

This page embeds the monthly ports catalog directly in HTML and keeps the browser experience searchable and paged without requiring a separate raw JSON action.

Return to the GeoIP downloads hub, browse the CIDR reference and root zone database, or review the monthly reference publication methodology.

Back to Downloads

Protocol and service context

This catalog provides a browser-friendly reference for protocols and ports so users can interpret service labels and transport mappings without leaving the site.

Useful for rules and troubleshooting

The embedded format is practical when checking firewall rules, application ports, or network service conventions while reviewing operational data.

Related GeoIP paths

Use the GeoIP database overview for the product view, or the downloads hub for release-based file access.

Browse and filter

Use the search field to filter the embedded catalog client-side. The browser view remains paged, with an upper page-size option of 10,000 rows for materially larger future catalogs.

The embedded ports catalog is ready.

Catalog notes

Catalog rows will render here from the embedded monthly publication.

How to use the Ports reference

This catalog provides a searchable browser view of service names, port numbers, descriptions, and transport support. It supports network-operations lookup work, but it also serves as an explanatory layer for visitors who need quick protocol context without leaving the site.

Use the table search to narrow down service names or port values, then rely on the transport summary and official markers to interpret the rows. This explanation clarifies the operational purpose of the page beyond the raw data table itself.
